text: hi everybody this is edited video of ultra low anterior resection using SI robot this is a forty five year old deposed NACTRT having a low rectal growth about three centimeter from the inner rectal ring we use a standard SI port set up for low anterior resection. A twelve mm assistant port is used from the right ear hippos and a five mm assistant port from the right hypochondrium. That's the deep, well-seen usage of the Robo with its magnification and mobility. We do a practical examination to confirm our margins, distal margins well beyond the palpable growth. Here we can see the magnification and the use of this endoveris technology put into use.

The surgeon mobilizes the rectal tube from the levator muscles, with the mesorectum coned and the vagina retracted anteriorly and superiorly. The right lateral and posterior dissections are performed, with magnification aiding in the identification of the rectal tube and levator muscles. The left lateral aspect of the rectal tube is further mobilized, revealing the cord, mesorectum, and bare rectal tube. A 60 mm stapler is applied from the 12 mm port in the right iliac fossa to secure the distal margins.

The surgeon begins with a medial to lateral approach, performing high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery. The mesocolon is completely mobilized, including the splenic flexure. The mesocolon is divided proximal to the inferior mesenteric pedicle, marking the proximal extent of the colon division. A six-centimeter Pfannenstiel incision is made to extract the specimen, followed by the application of the anvil to the proximal tube. The remaining rectal tube is visible.

The surgeon retracts the vagina anteriorly and superiorly, with the levator muscles dissected all around. The mesocolon is divided towards the marked proximal extent of the colon. An ileostomy is fashioned, and the post-operative situation is shown in a photo.
